_doc: The Company's service registry — the single self-describing source of truth for what runs on this machine. The AI reads this to know the whole landscape; the `company` command (ops/company) operates it. Adding a service = adding an entry here. It's OK if some entries are imperfect (ports/units) — better than the scatter it replaces. systemd is the muscle underneath; this is the map + the console. NOTHING auto-starts at boot anymore (2026-06-06, Tim's call): every service is on-demand via `company up`. The `autostart` flag now ONLY means 'started by bare `company up` (no target)' — kept true for the surface (canvas+bridge) so `company up` brings the interactive Company up; everything else is explicit (`company up brain|voice|<svc>`). `vram_mb` is an approximate resident/reserved cost used by `company` to surface GPU budget against the 16 GB ceiling — estimates, not measured. `serve` is the script whose default model `company swap` rewrites.

combos._doc: Named sets of services meant to run TOGETHER. `company up @<name>` starts the set (the resource manager still checks it all fits). Add a combo = add an entry here. Extensible — a combo can mix any services (models, voice, surface).

small-pair.note: the 2B + 0.8B chat tiers co-resident (gpu_util 0.45 + 0.30 ≈ 12.3 GB — VERIFIED both served at once 2026-06-06, fits alongside voice).

wake.note: the after-restart wake ritual (Tim, 2026-06-11): the surface's API + the resident brain/embedder pair + the default ear/voice — everything the phone PWA needs. ~12GB co-resident, verified together 2026-06-11. NOTE: the canvas (vite) is unmanaged — start it separately (cd canvas/app && npm run dev); making it a managed unit is a named future row.

xsession.note: The cross-session CHANNEL fabric loadout (Tim gave the GPU 2026-06-14): pplx-embed-context-4b (~8.2G) for semantic session-find/transcript-search + Qwen3-TTS VoiceDesign (~4.4G, no clip — the voice for spoken cross-session conversation) + whisper.cpp STT (CPU, 0 VRAM, the ear). ~12.6G GPU co-resident, fits the 16G card with headroom. The embedder+voice pairing Tim authorized to start (swap voice engine later via the registry). + rerank-jina (CPU /8008, 0 VRAM) — the rerank stage for cross-session recall.

groups.core: The live interactive Company — the surface + its API (the always-on heart)
groups.models: On-demand models — started per task, VRAM-budgeted, usually dormant
groups.reach: Network + always-on system services (the body's connection to the world)
groups.jobs: Scheduled timers — derived-data builders (no port; ◐ active = timer armed, next fire on schedule)

session-supervisor.title: Session supervisor — owns the supervised Claude Code fleet (Session Fabric F1)
session-supervisor.note: 127.0.0.1 ONLY (exposure law, audit B3); spawns claude subprocesses ON DEMAND (idle = owns nothing); the SINGLE writer of agent_sessions.* events; consumes agent_sessions/mail.jsonl intents. Cap COMPANY_FABRIC_CONCURRENCY (default 3) · per-turn watchdog COMPANY_FABRIC_TURN_TIMEOUT_S (default 900s) · permission COMPANY_FABRIC_PERMISSION (default plan). Operator face: `company session`. On-demand per the no-boot-autostart law.

chat-4b.title: Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ (vLLM) — a local WORKER (not the RHM brain; the RHM main is a cloud model)
chat-4b.note: config-driven via serve_model.sh (migrated from serve script 2026-06-06). Tune: `company config chat-4b <key> <value>`.

config._note: model-id NOT located on disk (kokoro runs but its weights id is unconfirmed) — left empty; flag for verification. Not cataloged (no verified id).

stt-whisper.title: Whisper.cpp STT (voice in — the boot-default ear)
stt-whisper.note: the live zero-install on-machine ear; serves the OpenAI-compat route /v1/audio/transcriptions (this build returns 404 for /inference). STT_DEFAULT=whispercpp. Registered in voice/stt.py STT_PROVIDERS.

stt-parakeet.title: NVIDIA Parakeet-TDT 0.6B v3 STT (GPU, NeMo)
stt-parakeet.note: managed as user-unit (flipped from manual 2026-06-06: cgroup teardown is orphan-safe; `company up/down` and voice/lifecycle agree). GPU ear — VERIFIED RUNNING 2026-06-05 (load→up ~30s, ~5.1 GB resident, transcribes; unload frees). Own NeMo venv (CUDA-13). Live multilingual workhorse → candidate STT_DEFAULT. See voice/ears/REQUIREMENTS.md.

stt-canary.title: NVIDIA Canary-Qwen 2.5B STT (GPU, NeMo)
stt-canary.note: managed as user-unit (flipped from manual 2026-06-06: cgroup teardown is orphan-safe; `company up/down` and voice/lifecycle agree). GPU ear — heaviest (~10 GB resident, near-OOM); on-demand only, never co-resident with a local brain. Own NeMo venv. English-max + understanding. See voice/ears/REQUIREMENTS.md.

stt-granite.title: IBM Granite Speech 4.0 1B STT (GPU, transformers)
stt-granite.note: managed as user-unit (flipped from manual 2026-06-06: cgroup teardown is orphan-safe; `company up/down` and voice/lifecycle agree). GPU ear — own transformers venv (COMPANY_GRANITE_DEVICE=cpu dodges the GPU hazard). Compact top-accuracy cross-check. See voice/ears/REQUIREMENTS.md.

tts-chatterbox.title: Chatterbox TTS (clone + intensity)
tts-chatterbox.note: trial engine (persona Viv). Clones COMPANY_VOICE_REF. ~3.4 GB. See voice/engines/REQUIREMENTS.md.

tts-orpheus.title: Orpheus TTS (named voices + cued emotion)
tts-orpheus.note: trial engine (persona Pip). vLLM-backed; ~10 GB + SWAP-HOSTILE (~17min cold) → pin/pre-warm, never cold-on-demand. See voice/engines/REQUIREMENTS.md.

config._note: engine (vLLM-backed, own unit + orpheus.py); config is the one source for gpu_util/max_model_len/model. NOT launched via serve_model.sh.

tts-cosyvoice.title: CosyVoice2 TTS (instruct + clone)
tts-cosyvoice.note: trial engine (persona Tess). Needs CUDA_HOME + COMPANY_VOICE_REF. openai-whisper pin conflict may block install (time-boxed). See voice/engines/REQUIREMENTS.md.

config._note: CosyVoice2-0.5B own repo — fixed footprint (vram_mb, no gpu_util). voice = persona voice_description (inference_instruct2).

tts-xtts.title: XTTS-v2 TTS (clone, realism ceiling)
tts-xtts.note: trial engine (persona Wren). Clones COMPANY_VOICE_REF; NON-COMMERCIAL license. ~2 GB. See voice/engines/REQUIREMENTS.md.

tts-qwen3tts.title: Qwen3-TTS VoiceDesign (describe a voice)
tts-qwen3tts.note: trial engine (persona Sable). VoiceDesign from a text description — NO clip needed (the trial starter). ~4.4 GB, ~25s cold. See voice/engines/REQUIREMENTS.md.

config._note: transformers VoiceDesign — fixed footprint (vram_mb, no gpu_util). voice = persona voice_description (instruct).

embed-bge.title: BGE-M3 embeddings (vLLM)
embed-bge.note: config-driven via serve_model.sh (migrated from serve script 2026-06-06). Tune: `company config embed-bge <key> <value>`.

embed-jina-v4.title: jina-embeddings-v4 multimodal (vLLM)
embed-jina-v4.note: CUSTOM server — NOT config-driven. vLLM lacks jina-v4 support, so it runs serve_jina_v4.py (raw transformers) in the jina-v4-env venv via serve_jina_v4.sh. Leave script-based.

config._note: config.model = JOIN/identity metadata ONLY — CUSTOM transformers server (see `serve`/`note`), NOT config-driven (vLLM lacks jina-v4 support; serve_jina_v4.py raw transformers). CORRECTED from an earlier 'confirm vLLM --runner pooling serves it' note, which contradicted the working custom server.

embed-pplx.title: pplx-embed-context-v1-4b (custom transformers embedder)
embed-pplx.note: CUSTOM server — NOT config-driven (like embed-jina-v4). Arch PPLXQwen3Model / model_type bidirectional_pplx_qwen3 is remote-code (trust_remote_code, auto_map→modeling.PPLXQwen3ContextualModel); vLLM 0.21 has no such arch (verified: absent from 356 supported archs, `vllm serve` fails at load_model) → runs ops/serve_pplx_embed.py (raw transformers) in ~/vllm-env via serve_pplx_embed.sh. OpenAI-compat POST /v1/embeddings; dim=2560; output is INT8 + UNNORMALIZED — compare via COSINE (model card). Contextual late-chunking model: flat `input` strings are embedded as single-chunk docs; optional `documents` (list[list[str]]) gives native per-chunk contextual embeddings. dtype bfloat16 (~8GB) — the card stores fp32. Interim transcript-search embedder (throwaway); ~10GB load authorized by Tim. Leave script-based.

config._note: config.model = JOIN/identity metadata ONLY. This is a CUSTOM transformers server (see `serve`/`note`), deliberately NOT config-driven. The context-4b IS served — the live transcript-search embedder, wired + verified by the retrieval session 2026-06-12. Set here (catalog session) so capabilities_for() resolves served_by. CORRECTED from a wrong earlier value (pplx-embed-v1-0.6b + a 'launch UNVERIFIED' note) — it is the 4b and it serves.

embed-jina-v5.title: jina-embeddings-v5 (vLLM)
embed-jina-v5.note: config-driven via serve_model.sh (migrated from serve script 2026-06-06). Tune: `company config embed-jina-v5 <key> <value>`.

chat-2b.title: Qwen3.5-2B chat (vLLM)
chat-2b.note: config-driven via serve_model.sh; tune with `company config chat-2b gpu_util <x>`. gpu_util 0.45 ≈ 7.2 GB; co-resides with chat-08b (combo small-pair).

chat-08b.title: Qwen3.5-0.8B chat (vLLM, ultra-concurrency)
chat-08b.note: config-driven via serve_model.sh; tune with `company config chat-08b gpu_util <x>`. gpu_util 0.30 ≈ 4.9 GB reserved / ~4.0 GB measured (0.20 was too low — starved the KV cache; the slice must hold weights + KV).

chat-nemotron.title: Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-AWQ (vLLM, text-only, tight)
chat-nemotron.note: config-driven via serve_model.sh (migrated from serve script 2026-06-06). Tune: `company config chat-nemotron <key> <value>`.

embed-qwen3.title: Qwen3-Embedding-8B (vLLM)
embed-qwen3.note: config-driven via serve_model.sh (migrated from serve script 2026-06-06). Tune: `company config embed-qwen3 <key> <value>`.

ollama.title: Ollama runtime (GGUF big models)
ollama.note: system-unit, kept enabled at boot (Tim's call). 0 VRAM idle; loads a model on request.

tailscale.title: Tailscale (tailnet + HTTPS serve to phone)
tailscale.note: system-unit, kept enabled at boot — the phone access path.

pipeliner.title: GitHub Actions self-hosted runner (Concept-Vi/ollama-pipeliner)
pipeliner.note: CI runner. On-demand as of 2026-06-06 (was boot-autostart). `company up pipeliner` when a CI run is needed; `company down pipeliner` to free it.

agent-sessions-exporter.title: Claude session transcripts → ~/corpora/claude-sessions markdown (every 20 min)
agent-sessions-exporter.note: F1.4 transcript memory. Managed unit = the TIMER (`company up agent-sessions-exporter` arms it; the oneshot .service fires every 20 min). READ-ONLY on ~/.claude; filter law + redaction in ops/agent_sessions_exporter.py. Timer is WantedBy=company.target so it rises/falls with the Company (no orphan). The SECOND stage of the transcript-search circuit (export → reindex) is `claude-sessions-reindex`.

claude-sessions-reindex.title: Reindex beat — substrate DELTA reindex of claude-sessions after each export (~5 min offset)
claude-sessions-reindex.note: INTERIM transcript-search (throwaway; Tim is building the real memory system elsewhere). The SECOND beat of the circuit: Claude jsonl → [agent-sessions-exporter] → corpus markdown → [this] → searchable index. Managed unit = the TIMER (WantedBy=company.target; fires *:05/20, ~5 min after the exporter's *:00/20). The oneshot .service runs ops/claude_sessions_reindex.py in the substrate venv (obsidian-overlord/.venv). CHEAP-BY-DESIGN: compares the corpus' newest .md mtime + file count against ~/corpora/claude-sessions/.reindex_marker.json and EXITS doing nothing (no model load, no GPU) when nothing changed — the common case. When transcripts changed it runs a substrate DELTA scan (sha256 skips unchanged files) of the `claude-sessions` vault embedded by the `embed-pplx` service into an ISOLATED state dir (~/.cache/company/substrate-claude-sessions, separate from the bge-m3 substrate). FAIL LOUD: if files changed but embed-pplx is DOWN it exits non-zero and leaves the marker stale so the next fire retries (`company up embed-pplx` first, or it can self-ensure with --ensure-embedder). Search face: ops/wire_substrate_claude_sessions.py search / ops/transcript_search.py (numpy fallback).

rerank-jina.title: jina-reranker-v3 listwise reranker (CPU endpoint)
rerank-jina.note: CPU endpoint (CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" — 0 VRAM, no GPU contention with the loadout). Surfaces ops/rerank.py:Reranker(jina-v3) as POST /rerank {query, candidates|texts:[str|dict], top_n?} -> {ranking:[{item,text,rerank_score,orig_rank,rank},...]} sorted desc; GET /health. Runs from ~/vllm-env (torch 2.11+transformers 5.9 already present — NO new install; checked per Tim 2026-06-14 'install after checking'). The rerank stage for cross-session semantic recall (runtime/session_recall.py) — bridge-free, served like embed-pplx :8007. Loads in ~5s; weights jinaai/jina-reranker-v3 in HF cache.
